turn_on.script:
import urllib.request
# Execute GET request to set level to maximum (254)
urllib.request.urlopen('http://192.168.1.220:9800/api/level?id=0x000000002ce4355f&level=254')

turn_off.script:
import urllib.request
# Execute GET request to turn off (0)
urllib.request.urlopen('http://192.168.1.220:9800/api/level?id=0x000000002ce4355f&level=0')

set_level.script:
import sys, urllib.request
# Send integer level (0-254) directly to the API
matter_level = int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 254
urllib.request.urlopen(f'http://192.168.1.220:9800/api/level?id=0x000000002ce4355f&level={matter_level}')

read_level.script:
import urllib.request, json
# Retrieve integer level directly from the API
res = urllib.request.urlopen('http://192.168.1.220:9800/api/level?id=0x000000002ce4355f')
data = json.loads(res.read().decode('utf-8'))
print(data.get('level', 0))

set_color_temperature.script:
import sys, urllib.request
# Set color temperature using Mired
mired = int(sys.argv[1]) if len(sys.argv) > 1 else 250
urllib.request.urlopen(f'http://192.168.1.220:9800/api/mired?id=0x000000002ce4355f&mired={mired}')

read_color_temperature.script:
import urllib.request, json
# Retrieve current color temperature in Mired
res = urllib.request.urlopen('http://192.168.1.220:9800/api/mired?id=0x000000002ce4355f')
data = json.loads(res.read().decode('utf-8'))
print(data.get('mired', 0))
